Je produis le rapport complet maintenant. J'ai assez de données.

---

# Audit complet : Back-Office ISNA V1 → ISNA Consolidated V2

## Chiffres clés

| | V1 (`isna_app`) | V2 Consolidated (`isna-opus`) | Écart |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Fonctions backend** | 165 Netlify + 34 Edge | 34 contrôleurs NestJS | **6× plus en V1** |
| **Pages frontend** | 398 | ~180 (apps/app) | 2× plus en V1 |
| **Composants** | 520 | ~100 (estimation) | 5× plus en V1 |
| **Modules API** | — | 28 enregistrés | Structure présente |

---

## Ce qui manque pour connecter le back-office V1 → V2

### 🔴 Bloc 1 — Studio LIRI (le plus gros écart)

Le Studio LIRI V1 contient **44 pages** sous `/studio/` qui n'existent PAS dans la V2 :

**Pages inexistantes en V2 :**
- `StudioLiriHubPage` — hub principal de l'écosystème LIRI (créer, reprendre, importer, diffuser, bibliothèque)
- `StudioLiriCourseBuilderPage` — Course Builder 10 étapes (LIRI Agent + blocs pédagogiques)
- `StudioLiriFormationBuilderPage` — Formation Builder (arbre temporel + éditeur + LIRI Assistant)
- `StudioSmartboardKonvaPage` — SmartBoard Designer v2 (canvas Konva, LONGIA, collaboration)
- `DesignerPostProductionDock` — Post-production (source, transcript, segments, NLE, pipeline)
- `StudioLiriMultilangPage` — Traduction multilingue live/vidéo
- `StudioLiriBibliothequePage` — Bibliothèque d'assets, templates, presets
- `StudioLiriPedagogieFuturPage` — Pédagogie du futur
- `StudioLiriStudioImagePage` — Canvas + IA contextuelle
- `StudioLiriEmbeddedControlPage` — Contrôle app intégrée
- `StudioLiriConstructeursHubPage` — Hub des constructeurs
- `StudioLiriConstructeursGuidePage` — Guide constructeur
- `StudioLiriImportPage` — Import documents/templates/assets
- `StudioLiriAgentPage` — Agent LIRI immersif plein écran
- `StudioIsnaPipelinePage` — Pipeline ISNA
- `StudioCourseBuilderPage` + `StudioCourseBuilderProPage` — Course Builder manuel/pro
- `StudioCourseLabPage`, `StudioFormationLlmBuilderPage` — Lab + LLM Builder
- `StudioLiveImmersivePage`, `StudioLiveLabPage`, `StudioLivePreviewPage` — Live immersif
- `StudioDebateBuilderPage`, `StudioDebateDetailPage`, `StudioDebatePrepPage` — Débat builder
- `StudioAdCreatorPage`, `StudioExportCenterPage` — Création pub + export
- `StudioCoachingPage`, `StudioAppointmentPage`, `StudioEventPage` — Coaching/rendez-vous
- `LiveArenaPage` — Arène LIRI
- `LivePostIntelligencePage`, `LivePreparationStudioPage`, `LiveWaitingRoomPage` — Live
- `StudioSmartboardHelpPage` — Aide SmartBoard
- `DocumentCoachPanel`, `StudioEntryPage`, `StudioRouter` — Navigation/coaching

**Backend manquant → 14 Netlify Functions + 16 Edge Functions LIRI :**

Netlify Functions à migrer :
- `liri-masterclass-factory.js` — génération de masterclass
- `liri-masterclass-document-analyze.js` — analyse document
- `liri-slide-generate.js` — génération slides
- `liri-summary-generate.js` — résumé IA
- `liri-mindmap-generate.js` — mindmap
- `liri-orchestrator-start.js` / `liri-orchestrator-status.js` — orchestrateur
- `liri-script-ai-improve.js` — amélioration script
- `liri-pedagogy-generate.js` — pédagogie
- `liri-neuronq-reformulate.js` / `liri-konva-course-copilot.js` — NeuroQ/Konva
- `smartboard-ia-generate.js` / `smartboard-mindmap-course.js` — SmartBoard IA
- `capture-studio-slide-ai.js` — capture slide

Supabase Edge Functions à migrer :
- `liri-smartboard-architect-structured/` — architecture structurée
- `liri-smartboard-designer-chat/` — chat designer
- `liri-smartboard-vision-describe/` / `liri-smartboard-vision-segment/` — vision
- `liri-agent-course-generate/` — génération cours par agent (appelé par LIRIAgent.jsx)
- `liri-coach-slide/` — coach slide
- `liri-designer-voice-realtime-session/` — voix temps réel
- `liri-formation-engine/` — moteur formation
- `liri-konva-scene-improve/` — amélioration scène Konva
- `liri-multilang-live/` / `liri-multilang-video/` — multilangue
- `liri-tts/` — synthèse vocale
- `liri-vision-temp-sweep/` — balayage temporel vision
- `studio-cover-prompt-assistant/` / `studio-longia-chat/` / `studio-longia-chat-stream/` — LONGIA

**Frontend features V1 non migrées :**
- `features/smartboard-konva-editor/` — éditeur Konva complet (store, canvas, composants, hooks, lib)
- `features/smartboard-designer/` — designer visuel
- `features/course-builder/` — course builder (composants, hooks)
- `features/nle-engine/` — moteur NLE (montage vidéo non-linéaire)
- `features/live/` — hooks + host pour live
- `features/export/` — services d'export
- `features/library/` — composants + hooks bibliothèque

---

### 🔴 Bloc 2 — Admin Dashboard (20 pages V1, 0 page admin en V2)

Pages admin V1 inexistantes en V2 :
- `AdminDashboard.jsx` — dashboard stats (users, contenu, activités, communautés)
- `UsersPage.jsx`, `StudentsPage.jsx`, `CreateUserPage.jsx` — gestion utilisateurs
- `BillingPage.jsx`, `PaymentsPage.jsx` — billing/paiements admin
- `AdminMarketingPage.jsx`, `MarketingToolsSuitePage.jsx` — marketing admin
- `AdminCommunitiesPage.jsx`, `ForumModerationPage.jsx` — communautés/modération
- `AdminCurriculumPage.jsx`, `AdminSecondYearCurriculumPage.jsx`, `AdminModulesManagementPage.jsx` — cursus
- `ContentEditor.jsx`, `IriBuilderPage.jsx` — contenu/IRI
- `AuditLogsPage.jsx`, `SettingsPage.jsx` — logs/paramètres
- `OwnerDashboard.jsx`, `OwnerAdminDashboard.jsx`, `TestAdminDashboard.jsx` — owner

---

### 🔴 Bloc 3 — Course Builder Pipeline + Post-Production

Backend V1 non migré (20 fonctions) :
- Pipeline : `course-builder-pipeline-auto-segment.js`, `course-builder-pipeline-master-script.js`
- Post-prod : `course-builder-postprod-version-list/save/restore.js`
- Render : `course-builder-render-enqueue/status/worker.js`
- Segments IA : `course-builder-segment-ai-approve/generate/regenerate.js`, `course-builder-segment-illustration-regenerate.js`
- Workers : `ai-generation-worker.js`, `annual-program-generate.js`, `post-call-report.js`, `reformulate-text.js`

La V2 a un `course-builder.service.ts` avec segmentation naïve — pas d'IA, pas de pipeline réel.

---

### 🔴 Bloc 4 — NeuroRecall (non migré du tout)

4 fonctions V1 :
- `neuro-recall-bootstrap.js` — initialisation session
- `neuro-recall-create-postprod-content.js` — contenu post-production
- `neuro-recall-generate-flashcards.js` — flashcards
- `neuro-recall-generate-node-reports.js` — rapports

La V2 a un module `neuro-recall/` avec squelette — pas de logique réelle.

---

### 🔴 Bloc 5 — Billing Multi-Provider

V1 gère **5 providers de paiement** avec 23 fonctions :
- Stripe : `billing/webhook-stripe.js`, `billing/create-initial-checkout.js`, `billing/create-renewal-checkout.js`
- Chariow : `billing/webhook-chariow.js`, `billing/chariow-attach-external.js`, `cimolace/cimolace-chariow-webhook.js`
- CinetPay : `billing/webhook-cinetpay.js`
- NOWPayments : `billing/webhook-nowpayments.js`
- PayPal : `billing/webhook-paypal.js`
- VirtuelMbolo : `billing/create-virtuelmbolo-subscription.js`, `billing/renew-virtuelmbolo.js`, `billing/get-virtuelmbolo-invoice.js`
- Gestion : `billing/run-renewal-cycle.js`, `billing/expire-subscriptions.js`, `billing/process-webhook-dlq.js`, `billing/save-tenant-billing-preferences.js`, `billing/save-tenant-payment-accounts.js`, `billing/get-tenant-billing-context.js`, `billing/payment-status.js`, `billing/subscription-status.js`, `billing/activate-license.js`, `billing/create-payment.js`

V2 n'a que **Stripe Checkout unitaire** (+ module Pawapay squelettique). Manque tout le multi-provider, les abonnements, les renouvellements, les licences.

---

### 🔴 Bloc 6 — Secretariat / Booking avancé

8 pages V1 :
- `SecretariatDashboard.jsx`, `SecretariatPortalPage.jsx`
- `SecretariatAppointmentsPage.jsx`, `SecretariatCalendarPage.jsx`
- `SecretariatInboxPage.jsx`, `secretariatInboxTriage.js`
- `OrgMailboxPage.jsx`, `SecretariatMarketingPanelPage.jsx`

V2 a un `booking.service.ts` avec CRUD slots/appointments + `secretariat.service.ts` — mais pas la boîte mail, pas le calendrier, pas les rappels, pas l'ICS.

---

### 🔴 Bloc 7 — Cimolace Back-Office propriétaire

V1 a 18 pages Cimolace :
- `CimolaceBillingDashboardPage.jsx`
- `CimolaceContactPage.jsx`, `CimolaceHostingPage.jsx`, `CimolaceInstallerPage.jsx`
- `CimolaceLandingCatalog.jsx`
- Sous-dossiers : `admin/`, `client/`, `company/`, `legal/`, `resources/`
- VirtuelMbolo : 10 pages (Dashboard, Booking, Elite, Starter, Pro, Payment, Subscription...)

V2 a un catalogue + backoffice modules NestJS, mais le backoffice propriétaire Cimolace (gestion clients, abonnements, billing dashboard) n'existe pas.

---

### 🔴 Bloc 8 — Live Avancé / Immersif

V1 a :
- `ImmersivePhoneCompanionPage.jsx`, `LiveMobileCameraPage.jsx` — mobile
- `LiveArenaPage.jsx` — arène immersive LIRI
- `LivePostIntelligencePage.jsx`, `LivePreparationStudioPage.jsx`, `LiveWaitingRoomPage.jsx`
- Fonctions : `immersive-livekit-*` (4 fonctions), `livekit-mobile-camera-*` (2 fonctions), `livekit-send-invitations.js`, `live-start-emails-scheduled.js`
- Composants : `live-studio/`, `live-room/`, `studio-pro/` — non audités en profondeur mais probablement 50+ fichiers

V2 n'a que le cœur MVP (create live, token, checkout).

---

### 🟡 Bloc 9 — Modules partiellement migrés mais incomplets

| Module | V1 | V2 | Ce qui manque |
|--------|----|----|---------------|
| **Forum** | 4 Edge Functions + composants | CRUD topics/posts/search | Modération, modération email, notifications forum |
| **Marketing** | 16 fonctions (campaigns, funnels, leads, automations, analytics) | CRUD promo/popup/bannière | Tout le growth engine : funnels, leads, analytics, automations, payment recovery, A/B testing |
| **Notifications** | mail-imap, resend-inbound, notifications push | Module squelette | IMAP sync, inbox mail, push temps réel |
| **Messaging** | response-engine (4 fonctions), chat live, immersive AI guide | Module squelette | Moteur de réponse intelligent, threads, inbox |
| **Growth** | analytics, payment recovery | Module squelette | Tableaux de bord analytics, recovery automatisé |
| **School Life** | `school-life/`, `school-life-admin/` composants | Rien | Vie scolaire, annonces, carnets, absences |
| **Teacher** | AnnualProgramPage, TeacherAgendaPage, TeacherPortalPage | Rien | Agenda prof, programme annuel, portail |
| **Student** | ModuleContentPage, StudentModulesDashboard + school-life | Rien | Dashboard élève complet, modules, progression |

---

## Synthèse : ce qu'il faut pour connecter V1 → V2

### Priorité P0 — Bloquant pour back-office fonctionnel
1. **Studio LIRI Hub** — page d'entrée `/studio/liri` avec navigation vers tous les constructeurs
2. **LIRI Agent (Course Builder IA)** — migration de `liri-agent-course-generate` Edge Function → NestJS Controller + Worker
3. **SmartBoard Konva Designer** — portage du canvas Konva + stores + LONGIA vers React/Vite V2
4. **Admin Dashboard** — stats, users, contenu, logs

### Priorité P1 — Essentiel pour parité V1
5. **Course Builder Pipeline** — segmentation IA, master script, render queue, post-production
6. **Billing Multi-Provider** — Chariow, CinetPay, PayPal, abonnements, renouvellements
7. **Secretariat complet** — calendrier, inbox mail, rappels, ICS
8. **NeuroRecall** — flashcards, postprod content, node reports
9. **Formation Builder** — arbre temporel, éditeur, LIRI Assistant

### Priorité P2 — Améliorations
10. **Live immersif** — arène, mobile camera, waiting room
11. **Cimolace Back-Office** — dashboard billing, gestion clients
12. **Marketing Growth Engine** — funnels, leads, automations, analytics
13. **Forum modération** + **Notifications push** + **Messaging**
14. **School Life** — vie scolaire, teacher/student portals

---

**Verdict :** La V2 consolidated a les fondations propres (tenant, auth, checkout, structure modulaire) mais **moins de 20% du back-office V1** est effectivement migré. Le plus gros chantier est le **Studio LIRI** (44 pages + 30 fonctions backend IA) qui constitue le cœur de la plateforme. Sans lui, le back-office V2 est une coquille administrative sans les outils de création pédagogique.

agents.result:
Now I have all 14 files fully read. Here is the structured audit:

---

### SUMMARY
Audited all 14 Netlify functions in BATCH 1 across LIRI Masterclass (10 functions) and SmartBoard + Studio Capture (4 functions). Each is a serverless POST endpoint calling AI (Claude/DeepSeek/Grok/OpenAI) via fallback chains and interacting with Supabase for persistence and authentication. The key finding: these map to **worker jobs** for long-running AI generation, **NestJS controllers** for lightweight request/response endpoints, and **Edge Functions** only for the ultra-light reformulation/direct-proxy cases. None require WebSocket or streaming architectural changes beyond what NestJS already handles.

---

### EVIDENCE

#### LIRI Masterclass

**liri-masterclass-factory.js** — `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/netlify/functions/liri-masterclass-factory.js:1-126`
- What: Generates a full LIRI course deck from source text using one of two pedagogical models (v1: 21-segment LIRI, v2: 26-segment "Productive Failure"). Returns structured chapters with segments.
- Inputs: `{ sourceText, lang?, courseType?, pedagogicalModel? }` (POST body). No auth.
- Outputs: `{ deck_title, subtitle, label, chapters[], provider, model_version, pedagogicalModel }`
- Deps: `aiChatClaudeDeepSeekGrok` (AI fallback chain), `_lib/liriMasterclassFactoryModel.js`, `_lib/liriFailureBasedCourseModel.js`. No Supabase.
- V2 mapping: **NestJS Controller endpoint** — single AI call, stateless, fits HTTP request/response.

**liri-masterclass-document-analyze.js** — `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/netlify/functions/liri-masterclass-document-analyze.js:1-400`
- What: Analyzes a raw document (up to ~52k chars with optional second-window) to produce a structured document with pedagogical topics, passages, concept dependencies, and coverage analysis.
- Inputs: `{ rawText, lang?, secondWindow?, gapFill? }` (POST). No auth. ENV: `LIRI_MASTERCLASS_SECOND_WINDOW`.
- Outputs: `{ structured_document, stats, truncated, analysis_quality, fragment_count, analysis_meta }`
- Deps: `aiChat` (with `claude-haiku-4-5` model), `jsonObjectMode`. No Supabase.
- V2 mapping: **Worker Job** — heavy processing (up to 3 AI calls: window1 + window2 + gapFill), potentially long-running.

**liri-slide-generate.js** — `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/netlify/functions/liri-slide-generate.js:1-220`
- What: Generates a single SmartBoard slide for a chapter, either from a named LIRI segment spec (precise layout) or a generic step (waiting/design/pedagogy/final).
- Inputs: `{ projectId, chapterId, segmentName?, step?, chapterTitle?, chapterSummary? }` (POST). No auth required.
- Outputs: `{ slide: { chapterId, layout, accentColor, elements[], state }, segmentSpec? }`
- Deps: `aiChat` (AI fallback), `_lib/liriSegmentsSlideSpec.js`, `getSupabaseAdmin` (reads/writes `liri_orchestrator_projects` table).
- V2 mapping: **NestJS Controller endpoint** — single slide generation, fast AI call, persists to DB.

**liri-summary-generate.js** — `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/netlify/functions/liri-summary-generate.js:1-119`
- What: Generates a post-session pedagogical summary from live session stats (duration, slides covered, questions asked/answered).
- Inputs: `{ sessionId, participantName?, durationSeconds, slidesCovered[], questionsTotal, questionsAnswered, questionsSkipped, questions[] }` (POST). Requires Supabase Auth (Bearer JWT).
- Outputs: `{ aiSummary, keyPoints[] }`
- Deps: `aiChat` (AI fallback), `getSupabaseAdmin` (auth + upserts to `live_session_summaries` table).
- V2 mapping: **NestJS Controller endpoint** — lightweight AI call, fast response, auth-gated.

**liri-mindmap-generate.js** — `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/netlify/functions/liri-mindmap-generate.js:1-100`
- What: Generates a mindmap JSON tree from a session summary + key points. Returns nodes with id/label/children/color.
- Inputs: `{ sessionId, summary?, keyPoints[], questions[] }` (POST). Requires Supabase Auth.
- Outputs: `{ nodes[] }` (tree structure)
- Deps: `aiChat` (AI fallback), `getSupabaseAdmin` (auth + updates `live_session_summaries.mindmap_nodes`).
- V2 mapping: **NestJS Controller endpoint** — fast AI call, structured output, auth-gated.

**liri-orchestrator-start.js** — `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/netlify/functions/liri-orchestrator-start.js:1-145`
- What: Bootstraps a 4-agent LIRI pipeline (Coach, Visual, SmartBoard, Quality). Extracts chapters from raw text via AI, initializes agent state, persists project to Supabase.
- Inputs: `{ rawText }` (POST). No auth.
- Outputs: `{ projectId, status: 'running', chaptersCount }`
- Deps: `aiChat` (AI for chapter extraction), `getSupabaseAdmin` (insert into `liri_orchestrator_projects` table).
- V2 mapping: **NestJS Controller endpoint** — kickoff call, inserts a job record, fast return. The actual agent work is done on polling via `liri-orchestrator-status`. The polling model is awkward; V2 should replace with a proper **Worker Job** queue.

**liri-orchestrator-status.js** — `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/netlify/functions/liri-orchestrator-status.js:1-181`
- What: Polls an orchestrator project's status. On each poll, simulates agent progress by advancing one pending chapter through processing→done via AI enrichment.
- Inputs: `?projectId=` (GET query param). No auth.
- Outputs: `{ status, agents[], chapters[], slides[], queue, logs[] }`
- Deps: `aiChat` (chapter enrichment via Coach), `getSupabaseAdmin` (read/update `liri_orchestrator_projects`).
- V2 mapping: **Worker Job** — This polling-based progression is anti-pattern. V2 should use a queue with workers processing chapters asynchronously; status becomes a simple read endpoint (**NestJS Controller**).

**liri-script-ai-improve.js** — `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/netlify/functions/liri-script-ai-improve.js:1-90`
- What: Improves/reformulates a course script passage using Claude directly (not the fallback chain). Supports 4 modes: improve, simplify, expand, transition.
- Inputs: `{ content, slideIndex?, context?, mode? }` (POST). Requires Supabase Auth. Hardcoded to `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` only (Claude Haiku 4.5).
- Outputs: `{ improvedContent }`
- Deps: `@anthropic-ai/sdk` (direct, no fallback), `getSupabaseAdmin` (auth only).
- V2 mapping: **Edge Function** or **NestJS Controller** — ultra-lightweight, single AI call, no DB writes. Edge Function is ideal for low-latency text transformation.

**liri-pedagogy-generate.js** — `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/netlify/functions/liri-pedagogy-generate.js:1-171`
- What: Generates the full 21 canonical LIRI pedagogical segments for a chapter (Objective, Competence, Knowledge, etc.) with fallback deterministic content.
- Inputs: `{ chapterTitle, chapterContent?, chapterObjective?, lang? }` (POST). No auth.
- Outputs: `{ segments: { "Objectif": "...", ...21 keys }, provider }`
- Deps: `aiChat` (AI fallback). No Supabase, no external APIs besides AI.
- V2 mapping: **NestJS Controller endpoint** — one AI call, stateless, fits HTTP request/response.

**liri-neuronq-reformulate.js** — `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/netlify/functions/liri-neuronq-reformulate.js:1-56`
- What: Reformulates a student's raw question into a clear pedagogical phrase (3rd person, nominal, max 2 lines).
- Inputs: `{ rawText, userName, sessionId }` (POST). Requires Supabase Auth. Max 600 chars input.
- Outputs: `{ reformulatedText, provider?, fallback? }`
- Deps: `aiChatClaudeDeepSeekGrok` (fallback chain), `getSupabaseAdmin` (auth only).
- V2 mapping: **Edge Function** — ultra-lightweight text transformation, low latency critical (live classroom), no DB writes needed.

**liri-konva-course-copilot.js** — `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/netlify/functions/liri-konva-course-copilot.js:1-82`
- What: Transforms source text into a structured Konva-renderable course document with slides (JSON output).
- Inputs: `{ sourceText, lang? }` (POST). Requires Supabase Auth. Max 48k chars input.
- Outputs: `{ course: { slides[] }, provider }`
- Deps: `aiChatClaudeDeepSeekGrok`, `_lib/liriKonvaCourseCopilotPrompt.js`, `getSupabaseAdmin` (auth only).
- V2 mapping: **Worker Job** — heavy AI processing (8k tokens, 48k char input), potentially slow. Should be async with status polling.

---

#### SmartBoard + Studio Capture

**smartboard-ia-generate.js** — `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/netlify/functions/smartboard-ia-generate.js:1-861`
- What: SmartBoard Architect — generates a complete professional slide deck (1037×750px) with master scripts from source text. Uses a 2-stage pipeline: (1) pedagogical analysis via AI, (2) structured JSON via OpenAI (or fallback AI chain). For short texts, uses a single-pass mode.
- Inputs: `{ sourceText, lang?, fast?, forceFullPipeline?, designCanvas? }` (POST). Requires Supabase Auth.
- Outputs: `{ slides[], master_scripts[], deck_title, format, theme, layout, provider, pipeline }`
- Deps: `aiChatClaudeDeepSeekGrok` (fallback chain for analysis), OpenAI API (`gpt-4o-mini` with `json_object` response_format for structuring stage), `getSupabaseAdmin` (auth only). ENV: `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `SMARTBOARD_OPENAI_MODEL`.
- V2 mapping: **Worker Job** — heaviest function in the batch (up to 3 AI calls, 861 lines of logic). Must be async with progress tracking. The pipeline mode with analysis→structure is a textbook worker pattern.

**smartboard-mindmap-course.js** — `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/netlify/functions/smartboard-mindmap-course.js:1-131`
- What: Generates a pedagogical course structure (double output: student SmartBoard + teacher MasterScript) with mindmap Mermaid diagram from source text.
- Inputs: `{ sourceText, lang? }` (POST). Requires Supabase Auth. Max 48k chars.
- Outputs: `{ deck_title, mindmap_mermaid, sections[], provider }`
- Deps: `aiChatClaudeDeepSeekGrok`, `_lib/liriPedagogicalCoursePrompt.js`, `getSupabaseAdmin` (auth only).
- V2 mapping: **Worker Job** — heavy single-pass AI call (8192 tokens), long input.

**capture-studio-slide-ai.js** — `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/netlify/functions/capture-studio-slide-ai.js:1-199`
- What: Multi-mode Capture Studio AI endpoint handling slide restructure (4-6 bullet points), transition text generation, and progressive build canvas generation.
- Inputs: `{ mode: 'slide'|'transition'|'progressive_build', title, points[]?, text? }` (POST). Requires Supabase Auth.
- Outputs (varies by mode):
  - slide: `{ title, points[], subtitle, core_idea, progressive_steps[], visual_type, graphic_style }`
  - transition: `{ text }`
  - progressive_build: `{ mode, slides[{ title, subtitle, core_idea, progressive_steps[], visual_type, graphic_style, speaker_script }] }`
- Deps: `aiChat` (AI fallback), `getSupabaseAdmin` (auth only).
- V2 mapping: Three distinct use-cases. Slide and transition modes → **Edge Functions** (fast, low-latency text transformation). Progressive build → **NestJS Controller** (slightly heavier but still one AI call).

---

### CHANGES
None.

### RISKS
- **Polling-based orchestration (liri-orchestrator-status)**: The current design simulates agent progress synchronously on each poll, which is fragile and blocks the HTTP response. V2 must replace this with a proper job queue (BullMQ/Redis) where each chapter processing is a discrete job.
- **Direct Anthropic SDK bypass (liri-script-ai-improve)**: This function bypasses the `aiWithFallback` module and calls `@anthropic-ai/sdk` directly. If the API key is missing or quota exhausted, there is zero fallback. Mitigate by routing through the unified AI service in V2.
- **No rate limiting or abuse protection**: None of these functions implement rate limiting. In V2, NestJS guards/throttlers should be applied, especially on the expensive AI generation endpoints.
- **Hardcoded CORS via respondToOptions**: Several functions use `respondToOptions` for preflight but it's inconsistent (some functions lack it). V2 should centralize CORS at the NestJS middleware/global level.
- **smartboard-ia-generate.js is 861 lines**: Monolithic. The analysis pipeline, structuring pipeline, normalization, fallback logic, and master-script alignment are all in one file. V2 should split into service classes (AnalysisService, StructuringService, SlideNormalizer, MasterScriptService).

### BLOCKERS
None.

Now I have read all 16 functions. Here is the comprehensive audit:

---

### SUMMARY
Audited all 16 Supabase Edge Functions in Batch 3 covering SmartBoard Architect/Designer, LIRI Engines, LIRI Multilang/Media, and Studio Longia. Each is a Deno-served HTTP endpoint with JWT auth, relying on a shared AI router (`aiChatClaudeDeepSeekGrok`) that chains Claude → DeepSeek → Grok → OpenAI fallback, plus direct provider calls (OpenAI Realtime, ElevenLabs, Google TTS, Groq, Gemini). Most functions are thin wrappers delegating to shared modules. V2 recommendation: nearly all should become NestJS controller endpoints (the interactive/streaming ones) or worker jobs (the scheduled sweep and CPU-heavy generation).

---

### EVIDENCE

#### SmartBoard Architect/Designer

- `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/supabase/functions/liri-smartboard-architect-structured/index.ts:1-153` — POST endpoint. Takes `assistantText`, `centralIdea`, `lang` from JSON body. Calls `aiChatClaudeDeepSeekGrok` with a structured system prompt (FR/EN) to extract structured SmartBoard items (normalized via `normalizeArchitectItems`). Returns `{ ok, items[], provider }` or error with parse/schema validation details. Uses `SYSTEM_LIRI_ARCHITECT_STRUCTURED_EN/FR` prompts, `parseJsonFromText`, `validateParsedRoot`, `normalizeArchitectItems`. **V2: NestJS controller endpoint.**

- `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/supabase/functions/liri-smartboard-designer-chat/index.ts:1-200` — POST SSE-streaming endpoint. Takes `messages[]`, `lang`, `context` (with optional `useRag`, `coach_architect_handoff`). Streams via `streamAiChatClaudeDeepSeekGrok`. Supports RAG via `fetchLongiaKnowledgeRagSnippets`, validates handoff via `validateCoachArchitectHandoff`. Uses `SYSTEM_LIRI_SMARTBOARD_DESIGNER_EN/FR`. Returns SSE `data: { text }` tokens then `data: { done: true }`. **V2: NestJS controller endpoint with SSE.**

- `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/supabase/functions/liri-smartboard-vision-describe/index.ts:1-215` — POST endpoint. Takes `imageBase64`, `mimeType`, `lang`, `centralIdea`. Tries Claude Vision (`claude-3-5-haiku-20241022`) first, then falls back to OpenAI Vision (`gpt-4o-mini`). Returns `{ description, provider }`. Direct Anthropic/OpenAI REST calls. **V2: NestJS controller endpoint.**

- `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/supabase/functions/liri-smartboard-vision-segment/index.ts:1-145` — POST endpoint. Takes `storagePath` (video in `liri-vision-temp` bucket), `centralIdea`, `lang`, `deleteAfter`. Downloads from Supabase Storage, base64-encodes, sends to Gemini (`gemini-2.0-flash`) with `inline_data` for video analysis. Returns `{ description, provider, model }`. Optionally deletes file after. Uses `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `GEMINI_VISION_MODEL`. **V2: NestJS controller endpoint (or worker for async processing).**

#### LIRI Engines (Formation, Coach, Konva)

- `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/supabase/functions/liri-agent-course-generate/index.ts:1-38` — Thin wrapper. Delegates entirely to `handleLiriCourseLlmRequest` with `LIRI_AGENT_SYSTEM_PROMPT` and `includePipelineMeta: false`. Supports pedagogical profiles: `maitre_pedagogue` (Claude), `architecte` (OpenAI), `cours_rapide` (Grok), `assistant_eco` (DeepSeek), `auto` (router). **V2: NestJS controller endpoint.**

- `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/supabase/functions/liri-coach-slide/index.ts:1-175` — POST endpoint. Two modes: `coach` (returns score+tier+analysis via `parseCoachScoreBlock`) and `architect` (returns structured redesign with tier: light/medium/deep/full via `systemArchitectForTier`). Takes `slideContent`, `lang`, `context`, `mode`, `architectTier`, `coachScore`, `coachArchitectHandoff`. Validates handoff. Uses `aiChatClaudeDeepSeekGrok`. **V2: NestJS controller endpoint.**

- `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/supabase/functions/liri-designer-voice-realtime-session/index.ts:1-97` — POST endpoint. Calls OpenAI `POST /v1/realtime/sessions` to create an ephemeral WebRTC session. Takes optional `voice`, `instructions`. Returns `{ client_secret, model, expires_at, id }`. Uses `OPENAI_API_KEY`, model `gpt-4o-realtime-preview-2024-12-17`. **V2: NestJS controller endpoint (token generation for client-side WebRTC).**

- `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/supabase/functions/liri-formation-engine/index.ts:1-30` — Thin wrapper identical in shape to `liri-agent-course-generate` but uses `LIRI_FORMATION_ENGINE_SYSTEM_PROMPT` and `includePipelineMeta: true`. Same multi-provider course generation pipeline. **V2: NestJS controller endpoint.**

- `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/supabase/functions/liri-konva-scene-improve/index.ts:1-140` — POST endpoint. Takes `objects[]` (Konva JSON), `canvas`, `sceneName`, `intent`. Calls `aiChatClaudeDeepSeekGrok` with a detailed SYSTEM_PROMPT as "Konva Layout Architect" to improve visual layout (positions, sizes, colors). Returns `{ objects[], canvas, suggestions[], provider }`. Auth is optional. **V2: NestJS controller endpoint.**

#### LIRI Multilang/Media

- `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/supabase/functions/liri-multilang-live/index.ts:1-46` — POST endpoint. Creates a live translation session via `startLiveSession(admin, userId, body)`. Takes `room_label`, `source_lang`, `target_langs`, `estimated_minutes`, `participant_hint`, `metadata`. Writes to DB, estimates credits. **V2: NestJS controller endpoint.**

- `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/supabase/functions/liri-multilang-video/index.ts:1-46` — POST endpoint. Creates a multilingual video project via `createVideoProject(admin, userId, body)`. Takes `title`, `source_lang`, `target_langs`, `duration_minutes`, `metadata`. Writes to DB, estimates credits. **V2: NestJS controller endpoint.**

- `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/supabase/functions/liri-tts/index.ts:1-98` — POST endpoint. Text-to-speech with tiered provider selection: `live` tier → ElevenLabs Flash v2.5 → Google Cloud TTS fallback; `export` tier → ElevenLabs Multilingual v2 → Google Cloud TTS. Takes `text`, `languageCode`, `tier`. Returns `{ audioBase64, mimeType, provider, tier, modelHint }`. Uses `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY`, `ELEVENLABS_VOICE_ID`, `GOOGLE_CLOUD_TTS_API_KEY`. **V2: NestJS controller endpoint (stateless synthesis).**

- `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/supabase/functions/liri-vision-temp-sweep/index.ts:1-43` — POST endpoint. Scheduled/manual cleanup job. Calls Supabase RPC `sweep_liri_vision_temp_objects()` via service role. Protected by optional shared secret header `X-Liri-Sweep-Secret`. Returns `{ deleted, ok }`. No user auth required. **V2: Worker job (cron/scheduled).**

#### Studio Longia

- `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/supabase/functions/studio-cover-prompt-assistant/index.ts:1-341` — POST endpoint. Multi-step AI art director for live cover/thumbnail image prompts. Three steps: `interpret` (analyzes user prompt → asks clarifying questions or delivers ready prompts), `chat` (ongoing Q&A), `finalize` (delivers final 3 prompts + quality score ≥85). Uses OpenAI `gpt-4o-mini` with `response_format: json_object`. Returns structured JSON with `refined_image_prompt_en`, `prompt_tiktok_9_16_en`, `prompt_poster_16_9_en`, `prompt_ad_4_5_en`, `quality_score`, etc. Enforces LIRI IMAGE PRO quality rules. **V2: NestJS controller endpoint.**

- `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/supabase/functions/studio-longia-chat/index.ts:1-256` — POST endpoint (non-streaming). LONGIA AI Hub chat with two modes: `coach` (Groq `llama-3.3-70b-versatile` → OpenAI `gpt-4o` fallback) and `architect` (Claude → DeepSeek → Grok chain via `aiChatClaudeDeepSeekGrok`). Includes auto-routing via `routeLongiaLlmMode`, LONGIA Core orchestration (`runLongiaOrchestration`), RAG via `fetchLongiaKnowledgeRagSnippets`, Konva context parsing, and Response Composer v1 (`composeLongiaPublishedResponseV1`). Returns `{ ...body, composed, routing }`. **V2: NestJS controller endpoint.**

- `/Users/ngowazulu/Downloads/isna_app/supabase/functions/studio-longia-chat-stream/index.ts:1-213` — POST SSE-streaming endpoint. Identical logic to `studio-longia-chat` but returns SSE text stream. Coach mode streams Groq → OpenAI; Architect mode streams Claude → DeepSeek → Grok via `streamAiChatClaudeDeepSeekGrok`. Same orchestration, RAG, routing. **V2: NestJS controller endpoint with SSE.**

---

### CHANGES
None.

---

### RISKS
- **Heavy AI coupling in Edge Functions**: Many functions (`studio-longia-chat`, `liri-coach-slide`, `liri-smartboard-designer-chat`) embed multi-provider AI chains directly. In V2, these should use a dedicated AI gateway/service abstraction rather than raw HTTP calls from controllers.
- **No rate limiting visible**: None of these functions implement per-user rate limiting. In V2, NestJS guards/throttling should be added, especially for expensive AI calls (TTS, vision, realtime sessions).
- **Shared module sprawl**: Functions like `liri-agent-course-generate` and `liri-formation-engine` are near-identical wrappers differing only in prompt and a boolean flag. V2 should consolidate into a single parameterized endpoint or service.
- **Secret management**: Multiple API keys (Anthropic, OpenAI, Groq, xAI, DeepSeek, ElevenLabs, Google Cloud, Gemini) are read from `Deno.env`. V2 NestJS should use a centralized config/secret service.
- **Hard timeout risk**: `studio-cover-prompt-assistant` has a 55-second AbortController timeout — could be marginal for complex multi-turn AI calls. Should be a worker job for long-running steps.

### BLOCKERS
None.
